rulings.Case Title: MAXIMILIAN H. MCKELVY VS. DIANA MEISTRELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant And Cross Complainant Diana Meistrells Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 19, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT DIANA MEISTRELL Defendant And Cross Complainant Diana Meistrells Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED, in part and DENIED, in part. (1) Cross-complainant's request to enjoin Cross-defendant from installing interior bolt locks is denied. Cross-complainant has not clearly explained how she is being locked out of the premises and how a dead bolt is different/worse from the locks she used to replace the locks installed by Cross-defendant. (2) Cross-complainant's request to enjoin Cross-defendant from continuing to use and maintain surveillance cameras in the common area is denied. The interim harm that Cross-complainant is likely to sustain if the injunction were denied is less compared to the harm that Cross-defendant is likely to suffer if the preliminary injunction were issued considering his legitimate safety concerns. (3) Cross-complainant's request to enjoin Cross-defendant from storing personal property in the common area hallway and the garden/patio area is granted, in part. Cross-defendant is enjoined from storing his bicycle in the garden/patio. The request is otherwise denied. Aside from the exercise equipment, which consists of a stationary exercise bike, Cross-complainant fails to specify what other items Cross-defendant allegedly "stores" in the garden/patio. Cross-complainant also fails to explain how his furniture, but not hers, blocks access to the patio area. (4) Cross-complainant's request to enjoin Cross-defendant from speaking to and/or making uninvited contact with Cross-complainant and her guests at the property is granted, with a modification. The request as presented in the moving papers is vague and over-broad. Cross-defendant is enjoined from confronting Cross-complainant's guests or renters, in Cross-complainant's presence or otherwise. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CEDRIC BERMOND VS. JOSE TORRES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 19, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT JOSE TORRES DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, in part, with leave to amend, and OVERRULED, in part. Demurrer to the First Cause of action for ejectment is overruled. Ejectment and unlawful detainer causes of action are not the same pursuant to CCP section 430.10(c). Furthermore, in its supplement brief, Defendant failed to provide authority supporting the proposition that abatement of a first-filed action is proper. Demurrer to the second cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained, with leave to amend. A remedy at law exists-unlawful detainer proceedings. (Filarsky v. Superior Court (2002) 28 Cal.4th 419, 433.) Demurrer to the third cause of action for violations of unfair competition law is overruled. Plaintiff has standing and sufficient facts. Demurrer to the fourth cause of action for unjust enrichment, and fifth cause of action for injunctive relief, are overruled. Sufficient facts alleged to state a cause of action. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
